terms.definition: React SDK providing prebuilt UI primitives (Player, Broadcast) for video experiences in web apps.
Streaming technique that detects viewer bandwidth in real time and switches between pre-encoded bitrate levels to maintain continuous playback.
Restricts who can view streams or assets via signed JWTs, API keys, or webhook authorization callbacks.
The top 100 orchestrators by total bonded stake that are eligible to receive work and inflationary rewards each round.
The process at the start of each round that determines the top 100 orchestrators by bonded stake to form the active set eligible to receive work.
Strategic bodies aligning ecosystem stakeholders on roadmap and opportunities through structured strategy-setting.
AES (Advanced Encryption Standard) in Cipher Block Chaining mode — symmetric encryption where each plaintext block is XOR'd with the previous ciphertext block before encryption.
A system that perceives its environment and acts autonomously to achieve goals, often powered by large language models with tool access.
Systems that perceive their environment and act autonomously to achieve goals, often powered by LLMs with tools.
The simulation of human intelligence processes by machines — including learning, reasoning, and problem-solving — using statistical models trained on large datasets.
REST API endpoint layer for routing AI inference requests through Livepeer's gateway nodes to GPU orchestrators on the network.
Running a trained neural network model on new input data to produce predictions or generated outputs, as opposed to the training phase.
End-to-end construct orchestrating data flow through processing steps to produce output.
Software component executing AI model inference tasks on an orchestrator node.
The container process that executes AI model inference jobs; go-livepeer communicates with it via HTTP and it loads models into GPU memory to process requests.
Portion of the Livepeer network dedicated to AI inference work, where orchestrators advertise AI capabilities and gateways route AI jobs.
Broad category encompassing AI-powered video processing tasks such as generation, transformation, and inference on live or recorded streams.
Special Purpose Entity funded by the community treasury to advance decentralized AI compute, scaling ComfyStream and BYOC pipelines.
JSON configuration file specifying available AI models including pipeline type, model ID, pricing, and warm status for an orchestrator node.
Smart contract registering AI service capabilities for orchestrators on the Livepeer AI network.
CLI flag starting a go-livepeer node as a dedicated AI worker process that connects to an orchestrator and handles AI inference jobs.
Community outreach initiative where participants represent Livepeer to new audiences.
Secret unique identifier sent with API requests to authenticate the caller and authorize access to platform resources.
Exploiting price differences between markets; in the Livepeer context, selecting lower-cost orchestrators when multiple are available for the same capability.
A Layer 2 Optimistic Rollup settling to Ethereum, processing transactions off-chain while inheriting Ethereum-grade security; the chain where Livepeer protocol contracts are deployed.
Stored video file (VOD) managed by Livepeer Studio, identified by a unique ID with associated metadata and playback URLs.
Authenticated Transfer Protocol — open decentralized social networking standard developed by Bluesky, enabling federated identity and data portability.
A guarantee that a set of on-chain operations either all succeed or none execute, preventing partial state changes.
AI pipeline converting spoken language audio into written text using deep neural networks.
AI system independently pursuing complex goals by perceiving, deciding, using tools, and acting without supervision.
Graphical representation of a user or AI entity, from 2D images to fully animated 3D digital characters driven by AI models.
Community-curated list of projects, tutorials, demos, and resources in the Livepeer ecosystem.
Bidirectional predicted video frames that reference both preceding and following frames to achieve the highest compression ratio in a coded video stream.
Running a trained model on a group of inputs asynchronously, optimising GPU utilisation through parallelisation.
Access token carried in an HTTP Authorization header, used by API clients to authenticate requests without re-sending credentials.
Number of bits conveyed per second of video; determines the data throughput rate of an encoded stream, directly affecting quality and file size.
Vision-language model by Salesforce using bootstrapped captioning and filtering for image understanding tasks including captioning and visual QA.
Unix timestamps embedded in each Ethereum or Arbitrum block header, used by smart contracts for time-dependent functions such as round boundaries.
A distributed ledger of records (blocks) securely linked via cryptographic hashes, managed by peer-to-peer consensus.
The total amount of LPT currently locked across the network through active bonding relationships between delegators and orchestrators.
Locking (staking) LPT tokens to an orchestrator in Livepeer's delegated proof-of-stake system to participate in network security and earn rewards.
The ratio of total bonded LPT to total token supply; Livepeer targets a 50% participation rate.
The core Livepeer smart contract managing all bonding, delegation, staking logic, fund ownership, and reward distribution on Arbitrum.
The Livepeer smart contract that tracks historical stake snapshots for governance, enabling stake-weighted voting power to be calculated at any past checkpoint.
Reward for completing a specific task, funded by community treasury or foundation.
Infrastructure connecting two blockchain ecosystems that enables token and information transfer between them.
The mechanism connecting two blockchain ecosystems to enable token or information transfer between them; specifically, moving LPT between Ethereum L1 and Arbitrum L2.
Legacy term for the node that published streams and submitted video jobs for transcoding; now replaced by 'Gateway.'
An internal go-livepeer component that manages active transcoding sessions between a gateway and its selected orchestrators.

terms.term: BYOC (Bring Your Own Compute / Bring Your Own Container)
terms.definition: Deployment pattern where users supply custom Docker containers for AI workloads on the Livepeer network, enabling arbitrary Python-based models to run as pipelines.
Coalition for Content Provenance and Authenticity — open standard producing tamper-evident manifests that record the origin and edit history of media files.
An AI service or job type — defined as a pipeline and model pair — that an orchestrator advertises as available to gateways for routing.
Mechanism by which orchestrators inform gateways of the AI services, pipelines, and models they can process.
Process by which a gateway routes an AI task to an appropriate orchestrator based on advertised capabilities.
A security mechanism where staking capital is required to participate, making Sybil attacks economically costly because each fake identity must fund real stake.
The degree to which staked capital generates productive returns through protocol inflation rewards, ETH fees, or work allocation.
Strategic vision for Livepeer to become the leading platform for real-time AI video pipelines, and the associated protocol upgrade enabling the AI inference subnet alongside transcoding.
Video encoding mode where the output data rate remains constant regardless of content complexity, trading compression efficiency for predictable file sizes.
A geographically distributed network of servers caching and delivering content with high availability and low latency to end users.
An on-chain snapshot of stake state recorded by the BondingVotes contract, used as the reference point for governance voting power calculations.
The on-chain action a delegator or orchestrator takes to collect accumulated inflationary LPT rewards and ETH fees that have been assigned to their stake.
A contract or system handling settlement of payments between gateways and orchestrators, including multi-user support, API key authentication, usage accounting, and fiat/stablecoin billing.
A text-based interface for interacting with software by typing commands, used to configure and operate Livepeer gateway and orchestrator nodes.
Short excerpt from a livestream or VOD asset defined by start and end timestamps, used for highlights or shareable segments.
Software or hardware that compresses and decompresses digital video, typically using lossy compression algorithms.
The latency incurred when an AI model must be loaded from storage into GPU memory before the first inference request, typically ranging from 5 to 90 seconds.
ComfyUI custom node running real-time media workflows for AI-powered video and audio on live streams; a Livepeer project integrating ComfyUI with the network's live streaming infrastructure.
Open-source node-based graphical interface for building and executing AI image and video generation workflows.
The combined percentage of inflationary rewards and ETH fees that an orchestrator retains before distributing the remainder to delegators.
Free, load-balanced, geo-distributed RPC service for Arbitrum L2 and Ethereum L1, maintained by the LiveInfra SPE.
The on-chain fund governed by LPT stakeholders via LivepeerGovernor, funded by a governable percentage of per-round inflation, used for ecosystem development.
Decentralized market matching GPU supply from orchestrators with demand from applications and gateways.
The risk arising when a disproportionate share of total bonded stake is held by a small number of orchestrators, reducing network decentralization and resilience.
CLI flags and environment variables that control node behavior including payment settings, pricing, preferred orchestrators, and network mode.
The production protocol upgrade (LIP-73) that migrated Livepeer's core protocol contracts from Ethereum L1 to Arbitrum One, significantly reducing gas costs.
Alternate name for the Confluence upgrade deploying Livepeer's core protocol contracts to Arbitrum Mainnet (LIP-73).
Specification for structured commit messages enabling automated changelogs.
The registry smart contract that manages all protocol contract addresses and coordinates protocol upgrades on Arbitrum.
A neural network architecture that adds spatial conditioning controls — such as edge maps, depth, or pose — to pretrained diffusion models.
HTTP mechanism that lets servers specify which origins outside their own domain are allowed to make browser requests to their resources.
Encoding quality control parameter that targets consistent perceptual quality by adjusting quantization per frame; scale runs 0–51 with lower values producing higher quality.
Fundamental building blocks that combine cryptography and economic incentives to enable secure, decentralized protocols.
NVIDIA's parallel computing platform and programming API enabling GPUs to be used for general-purpose processing and deep learning.
The primary general-purpose processor in a computer; in Livepeer, CPU handles node software overhead while GPU handles intensive transcoding and AI inference workloads.
An organization governed by smart contracts, with rules encoded in code rather than legal structures, and members vote on proposals via a decentralized ledger.
An adaptive bitrate streaming protocol that breaks content into small HTTP-served segments at multiple bitrate levels, allowing clients to switch quality dynamically.
Web-based management interface in Livepeer Studio for creating and managing streams, assets, API keys, and viewing analytics.
Livepeer's hosted realtime AI video platform turning live camera input into AI-transformed visuals with sub-second latency.
Distributed AI computation on blockchain networks enabling permissionless, trustless inference without centralized providers.
Transfer of control from a centralized entity to a distributed network, reducing single points of failure.
A marketplace of GPU resources contributed by orchestrators worldwide, coordinated through crypto-economic incentives for video transcoding and AI inference.
The act of LPT holders staking their tokens toward orchestrators they trust, sharing proportionally in rewards without running any infrastructure themselves.
A token holder who stakes LPT to an orchestrator to secure the network, participate in governance, and earn a share of rewards without operating infrastructure themselves.
Planned future Livepeer protocol phase focused on full decentralization with Truebit-based verification.
Consolidating requests from multiple users or applications for efficient routing to the orchestrator network.
A category of blockchain systems that incentivize communities to collectively build and operate physical or computational infrastructure using token rewards.
Funds (ETH) locked by a gateway into the TicketBroker smart contract to pay orchestrators via probabilistic micropayments.
Recurring meeting where core developers discuss protocol development, node releases, and roadmap.
Anyone building applications using Livepeer, typically through hosted services such as Livepeer Studio, Daydream, or Streamplace, or via library SDKs.
The abstraction layer providing tools, APIs, dashboards, and hosted services for building applications on top of Livepeer.
Set of SDKs, APIs, UI components, and hosted services for integrating video and AI capabilities into applications.
Generative models learning to produce data by reversing a gradual noising process applied during training.
Virtual replica of a physical object or system continuously synchronized with real-world data.
The reduction in proportional ownership experienced by non-staking token holders when new LPT is minted each round through inflation.
Distribution of encoded video segments across multiple orchestrator nodes for redundancy and parallel processing.
A deployment where a single gateway node operates both video transcoding and AI inference workloads simultaneously.
Blockchain analytics platform for SQL queries on on-chain data.
Livepeer's inflation model where the per-round LPT issuance rate adjusts up or down by 0.00005% each round based on whether staking participation is below or above the 50% target bonding rate.
An orchestrator's total active bonded stake, which determines their proportional share of inflationary rewards and their probability of being selected for job routing.
Special Purpose Entity bringing embodied avatar workloads (Live2D, Three.js, Unreal Engine) into Livepeer as intelligent public pipelines.
Learned numerical vector representation in continuous space where similar items map to nearby points, enabling semantic search and cross-modal reasoning.
Media file protected by encryption at rest so that only authorized parties holding the correct decryption key can access its content.
A structured set of video renditions at varying resolutions and bitrates for optimal cross-device adaptive bitrate viewing.
Specific URL path at which an API receives requests and returns responses for a defined operation.
Short-lived GPU resource allocation provisioned on-demand for a single AI inference task, released when the task completes.
The native cryptocurrency of Ethereum, used to pay transaction fees (gas) and as the fee currency for Livepeer payment settlements and orchestrator service fee payments.
Payments in Ether made by gateways to orchestrators for completed transcoding or AI inference work, distributed to delegators after the fee cut.
A decentralized, open-source blockchain with smart contract functionality; native cryptocurrency is Ether (ETH).
A 42-character hexadecimal string beginning with '0x,' derived from the last 20 bytes of a public key hash, used to send and receive funds and interact with smart contracts.
The layer where actual compute work is performed by orchestrators and workers, distinct from the on-chain protocol layer.
The payout amount assigned to a probabilistic micropayment ticket if it is drawn as a winner.
Automatic switching to a backup orchestrator or system when the primary fails or becomes unresponsive, maintaining service continuity.
A mechanism proving that an invalid state transition occurred on a Layer 2 chain, enabling challenges to incorrect rollup state roots.
Federation of social networking platforms that communicate via open protocols such as ActivityPub, enabling cross-platform interaction without centralized control.
The percentage of ETH service fees (or inflationary LPT rewards) that an orchestrator retains before distributing the remainder to delegators.
Accumulated ETH fees awaiting distribution between an orchestrator and its delegators, originating from winning payment ticket redemptions.
The portion of ETH fees earned by an orchestrator that is distributed to delegators proportionally to their bonded stake.
A governance-controlled mechanism that enables or adjusts the redirection of protocol fees to the community treasury or other designated destinations.
The condition where a blockchain transaction becomes irreversible and cannot be altered or rolled back.
Non-profit Cayman Islands Foundation Company stewarding long-term vision, ecosystem growth, and core development of the Livepeer protocol.
Frequency at which consecutive frames are captured or displayed, measured in frames per second (fps); common rates are 24, 30, and 60 fps.
Pattern in PyTrickle for building real-time video processing applications with custom per-frame processing logic.
Product by the MistServer team bridging Livepeer's transcoding infrastructure and real-world applications; provides libraries and integration tools for embedding Livepeer services.
The unit measuring computational effort required to execute operations on Ethereum or Arbitrum; users pay gas fees in ETH to cover execution costs.
A unit of digital storage equal to 1,073,741,824 bytes (binary); used in Livepeer hardware specifications for RAM, VRAM, and storage requirements.
A node that submits jobs, routes work to orchestrators, manages payment flows, and provides a protocol interface between applications and the Livepeer Network.
A hosted deployment model allowing users to access gateway functionality without managing their own infrastructure.
A pluggable logic layer inserted into the gateway request pipeline for custom processing such as authentication, rate-limiting, or request transformation.
Person or organisation running and maintaining gateway nodes for network access.
AI-produced video created by models that synthesize novel temporal frame sequences from text prompts or conditioning signals.
The initial 10 million LPT tokens created at protocol launch and distributed via the Merkle Mine mechanism.
NVIDIA's consumer-grade discrete GPU brand, encompassing the GTX and RTX product lines; the most common GPU family used by Livepeer orchestrator operators.
Official Go implementation of the Livepeer protocol containing the Broadcaster, Orchestrator, Transcoder, Gateway, and Worker roles in a single binary.
System of rules and processes for protocol decision-making including on-chain voting via LPT stake weight and the LivepeerGovernor contract.
The Livepeer Forum's governance category for LIPs, pre-proposals, and protocol governance discussions before on-chain votes.
The on-chain governance smart contract (LivepeerGovernor) that authorizes protocol upgrades and parameter changes via stake-weighted voting.
Meta-governance SPE ensuring transparent management of Livepeer governance and treasury allocation.
Open-source visualization and dashboarding platform for metrics and logs.
Non-repayable allocation from the community treasury or Livepeer Foundation for ecosystem development contributions.
High-performance remote procedure call framework using HTTP/2 and Protocol Buffers for efficient binary communication between services.
An ordered arrangement of I-frames and inter-frames within a coded video stream; the set of frames between keyframes.
Specialized processor for parallel computation used in rendering, video encoding, and AI inference workloads.
An orchestrator operator contributing GPU hardware and AI model capacity to the Livepeer network for transcoding or AI inference workloads.
A Livepeer node with GPU hardware that performs transcoding or AI inference tasks for an orchestrator.
NVIDIA's previous-generation consumer GPU product line; capable of Livepeer video transcoding but lacks the Tensor cores of the RTX series needed for accelerated AI inference.
Gateway Wizard SPE building a managed DevOps tool for running and managing gateway infrastructure.
Strict filter that immediately disqualifies orchestrators failing a required criterion such as exceeding the gateway's maximum price threshold.
Apple's HTTP Live Streaming protocol that encodes video into multiple quality levels segmented into small files, delivered with an index playlist (.m3u8) for adaptive bitrate playback.
Receiving a live video stream via HTTP push (rather than RTMP) for transcoding or AI processing.
An AI platform and open-source community providing model repositories, datasets, and inference APIs; a primary source for AI models deployed on Livepeer orchestrator nodes.
A fundraising mechanism where a blockchain project sells newly created tokens to the public in exchange for established cryptocurrencies.
AI pipeline transforming an input image into a modified output image guided by a text prompt or conditioning signal.
AI pipeline generating a textual description from an input image, encompassing captioning and optical character recognition.
AI pipeline generating a short video clip conditioned on a single input image, animating a still frame into motion.
Running a trained model on new input data to produce predictions or generated outputs, as opposed to the training process.
The dynamic issuance of new LPT tokens each protocol round, distributed to active orchestrators and delegators proportional to their bonded stake.
The fixed per-round rate (0.00005%) by which the inflation rate increases or decreases based on whether the current Bonding Rate is below or above the Target Bonding Rate.
Livepeer's algorithmic mechanism that adjusts the per-round LPT issuance rate dynamically based on the gap between the current Bonding Rate and the 50% Target Bonding Rate.
The per-round percentage of the total LPT supply that is newly minted and distributed to active orchestrators and delegators; adjusts dynamically via the Inflation Adjustment mechanism.
Newly minted LPT tokens distributed each round proportionally to active orchestrators and their delegators based on bonded stake.
Process of receiving a live video stream from a broadcaster's encoder into a media server, typically over RTMP, SRT, or WebRTC/WHIP.
Digital content dynamically responding to user input in real time, combining text, images, audio, and video.
A compensation model where payment tickets are sent at regular time intervals rather than per segment or per request.
The minting of new LPT tokens each round as the mechanism for distributing inflationary rewards to protocol participants.
A unit of work submitted to the Livepeer network specifying stream ID, transcoding options or AI pipeline, and price.
The sequence of stages from job submission through orchestrator selection, work execution, verification, and payment settlement.
Lightweight, human-readable data interchange format using key-value pairs and ordered lists, widely used for API request and response bodies.
A high-level interpreted scripting language used for web and server-side development; Livepeer's primary SDKs and gateway clients expose JavaScript/TypeScript APIs.
Compact, URL-safe token format carrying signed claims used for stateless authentication; in video access control, a signed JWT proves viewer entitlement without a server round-trip for every request.
Distance in frames or seconds between consecutive keyframes (I-frames); a shorter interval improves seeking accuracy while a longer interval improves compression efficiency.
Know Your Customer — identity verification process for regulatory compliance, requiring users to provide identifying information before accessing certain features.
Know Your Orchestrator — Live Pioneers interview series profiling active orchestrators on the network.
The Ethereum mainnet contract that holds LPT in escrow during cross-chain bridging to Arbitrum, locking L1 tokens as L2 equivalents are minted on Arbitrum.
The bridge contract deployed on Arbitrum that enables LPT token transfers between Ethereum L1 and Arbitrum L2.
Delay between capture at source and display on the viewer's device, accumulating at every pipeline stage.
The base blockchain network (e.g. Ethereum) that validates and finalizes transactions without reliance on another network.
A separate blockchain extending a Layer 1 by handling transactions off-chain while inheriting the security guarantees of the base chain.
Formal design document proposing changes to the Livepeer protocol, governance parameters, or ecosystem standards; the primary mechanism for protocol evolution.
Livepeer Improvement Proposal introducing the on-chain LivepeerGovernor contract, community treasury mechanism, stake-weighted voting, and the 10-round voting period.
Livepeer Improvement Proposal bundling the treasury establishment mechanism and defining the inflation-funded treasury reward cut rate.
Livepeer Improvement Proposal defining the AI model registry and capability discovery mechanism for the network.
SPE providing ongoing ecosystem infrastructure contributions with monthly progress updates.
Real-time AI processing applied to live video streams, typically using pipelines such as live-video-to-video running at interactive frame rates.
Independent community for long-term LPT holders producing educational content and guides.
AI pipeline applying generative models to a continuous video stream frame-by-frame at interactive frame rates.
SPE providing free, reliable blockchain infrastructure services including the Community Arbitrum Node.
JavaScript/TypeScript library for the Livepeer AI API enabling AI inference integration.
Python library for the Livepeer AI API providing programmatic access to AI inference pipelines.
Go server-side SDK for the Livepeer Studio API.
JavaScript library for the Livepeer Studio API providing programmatic access to stream and asset management.
An open-source Python reference implementation of a Livepeer gateway, enabling job submission, payment flow management, and pipeline routing from Python applications.
A participant in the Livepeer protocol or network — human or machine — that performs a defined role such as submitting jobs, providing compute, verifying work, or securing the system.
Platform by the Livepeer Cloud SPE increasing network accessibility with a free community AI gateway and managed developer services.
The full set of projects, tools, participants, and organizations building on or contributing to the Livepeer network.
Official protocol explorer for viewing orchestrator state, staking data, governance proposals, and delegating LPT.
Non-profit Cayman Islands Foundation Company stewarding long-term vision, ecosystem growth, grant programs, and core development of the Livepeer network.
Original company that built Livepeer's initial protocol architecture and core software.
The live operational decentralized system of orchestrators, workers, gateways, and broadcasters performing video transcoding and AI inference jobs.
The on-chain ruleset and smart contract logic governing staking, delegation, inflation, orchestrator selection, slashing, and probabilistic payments.
Hosted developer platform providing APIs, SDKs, and a dashboard for adding live and on-demand video experiences to applications, backed by the Livepeer Network.
JavaScript library for the Livepeer API providing programmatic access to Studio features including stream and asset management.
The OpenZeppelin-based on-chain governor contract for Livepeer that enables stake-weighted voting on protocol proposals using checkpointed BondingVotes data.
The core Go struct in go-livepeer representing a running Livepeer node instance, encapsulating configuration, session state, and network connections.
Real-time or near-real-time transmission of video and audio over a network to viewers as it is captured, without pre-recording.
Large language model — neural network trained on massive text corpora to understand and generate natural language.
Horizontally scalable log aggregation system by Grafana Labs, used in Livepeer orchestrator monitoring stacks.
Low-Rank Adaptation — parameter-efficient fine-tuning technique injecting trainable low-rank matrices into transformer layers to specialise a model without full retraining.
A system characteristic where the delay between an event occurring and a response being delivered is minimised; in Livepeer, sub-500ms round-trip times are targeted for real-time AI video pipelines.
The ERC-20 governance and staking token used to coordinate, incentivize, and secure the Livepeer Network.
New LPT tokens minted each protocol round via inflation, distributed to active orchestrators and their delegators.
An open-source media server library providing live video functionality including RTMP ingest and HLS output, used as the foundation for Livepeer transcoding nodes.
The primary public production blockchain where actual-value transactions occur on the distributed ledger.
The decentralized market on the Livepeer network matching gateway demand with orchestrator supply, governed by capability, price, and performance.
CLI flag setting the maximum transcoding price per pixelsPerUnit that a gateway will accept from an orchestrator; orchestrators above this threshold are excluded.
A CLI configuration flag setting the maximum price a gateway will pay for a specific AI pipeline/model pair (capability), overriding the general MaxPricePerUnit for that task type.
A CLI configuration flag setting the maximum price per pixelsPerUnit that a gateway will accept from orchestrators; orchestrators priced above this threshold are excluded.
Livepeer's algorithm for decentralized token distribution at genesis using Merkle proofs to fairly distribute initial LPT supply without an ICO.
Small-value payments for streaming services, implemented in Livepeer via a probabilistic lottery scheme to minimize on-chain transaction costs.
A small-value signed data structure sent from a gateway to an orchestrator representing a probabilistic payment; only winning tickets are redeemed on-chain.
Funding released incrementally upon achievement of predefined deliverables rather than in a lump sum.
The smart contract responsible for minting new LPT tokens during orchestrator reward calls and holding ETH collected from winning payment tickets.
Open-source media server providing live video ingest, transcoding, and delivery capabilities, used within Livepeer's infrastructure to handle protocol translation and stream routing.
Mathematical structure (neural network with learned weights) enabling predictions or content generation for new inputs, identified by a model ID and pipeline type.
Standardised documentation describing a model's intended use, training data, evaluation metrics, and known limitations.
Unique string identifier specifying which AI model to invoke on a repository hub, for example `stabilityai/stable-diffusion-xl-base-1.0`.
Status indicating whether an AI model is currently loaded in GPU memory (warm) or must be loaded from storage on demand (cold).
MPEG-4 Part 14 digital multimedia container format for storing video, audio, subtitles, and still images in a single file.
AI systems capable of processing and integrating multiple data types — such as text, images, audio, and video — for cross-modal understanding and generation.
Simultaneous restreaming of a single live input to multiple external destination platforms (e.g., YouTube, Twitch) in a single broadcast session.
Network-as-a-Product — a reference architecture and implementation for multi-tenant gateway operation providing JWT-based authentication, developer API keys, and per-user usage tracking.
The phenomenon where a network's value increases as more participants join, creating compounding benefits for all existing members.
Computing device connected to a network participating in protocol operations such as transcoding, routing, or staking; any computing device running Livepeer software.
A staking model in which users retain control of their private keys and token ownership while their LPT is bonded, so they are never required to transfer custody to a third party.
Non-player character not controlled by a human, increasingly powered by AI for dynamic, real-time interactions.
NVIDIA hardware video decoder that offloads video decoding from the CPU to dedicated silicon on NVIDIA GPUs.
NVIDIA hardware video encoder that offloads H.264 and H.265 encoding from the CPU to dedicated silicon on NVIDIA GPUs.

Architectural separation of the Orchestrator and Transcoder (Worker) processes, typically running on different machines, where the orchestrator handles protocol interaction and the transcoder handles GPU compute.
Free, open-source application for screen capture and live streaming, supporting RTMP, RTMPS, SRT, and WebRTC output protocols.
Activities occurring outside the main blockchain, typically for scalability, speed, or cost reasons, with results optionally settled on-chain.
A gateway node that operates without blockchain integration, using a remote signer for payment operations and specifying orchestrators manually rather than relying on protocol discovery.
Open-source tool for running large language models locally with a CLI and OpenAI-compatible REST API.
Actions, computations, or data that are directly recorded, executed, and verified on the blockchain with full transparency and security guarantees.
A gateway node connected to the Livepeer protocol on Arbitrum, managing its own Ethereum wallet and using on-chain probabilistic micropayments for orchestrator settlement.
Protocol-governed pool of LPT funded by inflation for community-approved ecosystem development, administered via the LivepeerGovernor contract.
Software whose source code is freely available for anyone to view, use, modify, and redistribute.

The deployment configuration that determines how a gateway connects to the Livepeer network: on-chain (Arbitrum-based payments, protocol discovery) or off-chain (remote signer, manual orchestrator addresses).
The competitive ecosystem of orchestrators offering differentiated services to gateways and delegators, distinguished by price, performance, reliability, and commission rates.
Supply-side operator contributing GPU resources, receiving jobs from gateways, performing transcoding or AI inference, and earning ETH fees and inflationary LPT rewards.
The process by which a gateway finds and evaluates available orchestrators — either automatically via the on-chain ServiceRegistry or manually via configured -orchAddr flags.
Data structure advertised by orchestrators containing capabilities, pricing, service URI, and metadata used by gateways for selection decisions.
Shared secret used to authenticate communication between an orchestrator process and its standalone transcoder or worker nodes in an O-T split deployment.
The Open Systems Interconnection reference model that defines seven network layers (physical through application) as a conceptual framework for understanding protocol design.
Predefined set of encoding parameters (resolution, bitrate, codec, frame rate) defining a single rendition of a transcoded video.
Additional operational costs beyond direct computation, including gas fees for ticket redemption, bandwidth, and administrative costs.
Minimum 'for' vote percentage (excluding abstentions) required for a governance proposal to pass.

Livepeer's pricing model where orchestrators are paid based on the total number of pixels transcoded, enabling granular and standardized cost comparison across different video resolutions and durations.
An off-chain mechanism where two parties conduct multiple transactions and only settle the final state on-chain, reducing per-transaction gas costs.
Signed off-chain data structure issued by a gateway to an orchestrator representing a probabilistic payment; only winning tickets are redeemable on-chain for their ETH face value.
Inflationary LPT and ETH fees that have been earned through staking but not yet claimed by calling the claim earnings function.
Livepeer's unit-based pricing mechanism where fees are calculated based on the number of pixels processed during a transcoding or AI inference job.
A cost model charging for transcoding work based on the total number of pixels processed (width × height × frame count), enabling standardized comparison across resolutions.
A cost model charging per individual AI inference request rather than per pixel, used for AI pipeline jobs where pixel count is not a meaningful unit.
The Livepeer protocol's fundamental time unit, approximately equal to one day of Ethereum blocks; reward minting, activations, and delegator earnings accrue on a per-round basis.
Composite metric rating an orchestrator's reliability and speed, calculated as latency score multiplied by success rate, used by gateways in orchestrator selection.
Property where anyone can participate without requiring approval from a central authority.
Single point in a video frame used as the fundamental pricing unit for transcoding work on the Livepeer network.
CLI parameter defining the number of pixels constituting one billable work unit, allowing granular pricing control.
A configured end-to-end AI processing workflow defining input type, model, and output, routed by the gateway to capable orchestrators.
Public identifier for retrieving playback URLs for a stream or asset without exposing the private stream key or internal asset ID.
Access rules (public or JWT-required) attached to a stream or asset that determine what authentication viewers must present before playback is allowed.
Livepeer's embeddable video player component (lvpr.tv) with built-in support for HLS adaptive bitrate streaming and WebRTC low-latency fallback.
Lottery-based payment scheme where gateways send signed tickets to orchestrators and only winning tickets are redeemed on-chain, amortising transaction costs across many payments.
Group of transcoder or worker nodes coordinated under a single orchestrator for increased capacity and redundancy.
Entity running an orchestrator that coordinates a pool of transcoder or worker nodes, managing on-chain operations and distributing earnings to workers.
Individual machine within an orchestrator pool, running go-livepeer in transcoder mode and executing GPU compute jobs delegated by the pool operator's orchestrator.
Informal governance discussion document posted on the Forum before a formal LIP or treasury proposal.
External data source providing real-time ETH/USD exchange rates used by orchestrators to denominate prices in USD terms.
Fundamental pricing unit for transcoding: cost in wei for processing one pixel of video.
CLI flag setting the price per unit for a specific AI pipeline and model pair, overriding the default pricePerUnit for that capability.
JSON configuration allowing orchestrators to set customised per-gateway-address pricing, enabling different rates for specific gateway partners.
CLI flag setting the transcoding price in wei per pixelsPerUnit that an orchestrator advertises to gateways.
A lottery-based payment scheme where only winning tickets are redeemed on-chain, amortizing transaction costs across many small payments without requiring per-payment gas.
An output specification defining a single transcoding rendition: resolution, bitrate, codec, and frame rate.
Open-source monitoring system collecting time-series metrics via HTTP pull from instrumented targets.
Model where participants prove they performed useful work for the network rather than just staking capital.
A blockchain consensus mechanism where validators stake cryptocurrency as collateral to propose and validate blocks, replacing computation-intensive proof-of-work.
The minimum bonded LPT balance (100 LPT) required to submit a formal on-chain governance proposal.
On-chain layer governing staking, delegation, rewards, and verification via smart contracts deployed on Arbitrum.
Verified chain of custody and edit history of a digital asset, confirming its origin and tracking modifications over time.
Open-source deep learning framework providing GPU-accelerated tensor computation and automatic differentiation, developed by Meta.
Python package for real-time video and audio streaming with custom processing, built on the Livepeer Trickle protocol.
A public goods funding mechanism where matching funds amplify small individual contributions so that projects with broad community support receive disproportionately larger allocations.
An ordered set of encoding profiles from lowest to highest quality used for adaptive bitrate rendition selection in video delivery.
The minimum amount of participating stake required for a governance vote to be considered binding and valid.
Video delivery or AI processing with latency low enough for bidirectional interaction, typically under 500ms via WebRTC.
Re-staking tokens that are in the unbonding period to an orchestrator, canceling the unbonding process and returning them to active bonded stake.
Rebuffering duration divided by total playback duration, expressing the fraction of viewing time spent waiting for the player to buffer data.
Stored archive of a live stream session automatically saved as a VOD asset when recording is enabled on the stream object.
Service or entity submitting a winning probabilistic micropayment ticket to the TicketBroker contract to claim its face value in ETH.
The on-chain process of cashing in a winning probabilistic micropayment ticket for its face value in ETH via the TicketBroker contract.
A service that holds private keys securely in an isolated environment and signs transactions on behalf of a Livepeer gateway or orchestrator node.
Single encoded version of a source video at a specific resolution, bitrate, and codec configuration, produced during transcoding.
A measure of an orchestrator's performance, reliability, and trustworthiness that influences job routing priority and payment selection by gateways.
ETH held as collateral in the TicketBroker contract backing outstanding probabilistic payment tickets; used if the gateway's deposit is depleted.
Pixel dimensions of a video frame expressed as width × height (e.g., 1920×1080); common tiers are 360p, 480p, 720p, 1080p, and 4K.
Architectural style for distributed hypermedia systems using standard HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) for stateless resource interaction.
A funding model that rewards past contributions or completed projects based on demonstrated impact, reducing speculative risk for the treasury.
Combination of inflationary LPT and ETH fees earned by orchestrators and delegators each protocol round.
The on-chain transaction (Reward()) that an active orchestrator must submit each round to mint and distribute new LPT inflation rewards to itself and its delegators.
The percentage of inflationary LPT rewards that an orchestrator retains before distributing the remainder to its delegators.
Formal solicitation posted by the community or Foundation inviting teams to submit proposals for defined work.
Layer-2 scaling solutions that execute transactions off-chain and post compressed data or proofs to Layer 1 to inherit its security guarantees.
Multi-participant WebRTC video session managed by Livepeer Studio, enabling multiple users to simultaneously broadcast and receive audio and video.
A discrete time interval defined in Arbitrum/Ethereum blocks during which staking rewards are calculated, the active set is determined, and protocol state is updated; approximately one day.
The Livepeer smart contract that tracks round progression, stores the current round number, and coordinates round-based protocol state transitions.
Protocol allowing a program to execute a procedure on a remote server; in blockchain contexts, the mechanism for querying and submitting transactions to a node.
TCP-based protocol for streaming audio, video, and data over a network, operating on port 1935; the dominant ingest protocol for live broadcasting software.
RTMP transported over a TLS/SSL connection, adding encryption to protect live video streams and metadata during ingest.
NVIDIA's current consumer GPU product line featuring dedicated Tensor cores that accelerate AI/ML inference workloads; RTX GPUs are well-suited for Livepeer AI pipeline tasks.
Meta's unified foundation model for promptable segmentation in images and videos with streaming memory, enabling interactive region selection.
Algorithm controlling the denoising process in diffusion models by defining the noise schedule and update rule for each generation step.
Ability to handle increasing workload by adding resources without degradation in performance or reliability.
Increasing gateway capacity to handle more concurrent requests, either horizontally (deploying additional gateway nodes) or vertically (adding resources to an existing node).
Stable Diffusion XL — advanced text-to-image model with a 3× larger UNet and dual text encoders, generating images at 1024×1024 resolution.
Collection of tools, libraries, and documentation enabling developers to build applications that integrate with a platform's APIs.
A time-sliced chunk of multiplexed audio and video data that is independently transcoded for parallel processing in Livepeer's pipeline; typically 2–10 seconds for HLS delivery.

(1) Video: the process of dividing a continuous video stream into short discrete chunks for HTTP-based delivery. (2) AI: task partitioning a digital image into regions by assigning a semantic label to every pixel.
A deployment model in which the operator runs their own infrastructure rather than relying on a managed cloud service; Livepeer gateways and AI nodes can be self-hosted on any compatible hardware.
A markup that gateway operators add on top of orchestrator costs when reselling gateway access to end users.
The on-chain registered endpoint URL that gateways use to discover and establish a connection with an orchestrator node.
A smart contract on Arbitrum where orchestrators register their service URI so that on-chain gateways can discover and contact them.
An active connection between a gateway and an orchestrator during which one or more jobs are processed within a continuous work period.
The on-chain finalization of off-chain payment obligations via ticket redemption through the TicketBroker contract.
Public/private cryptographic keypair used to sign and verify JWTs that gate access to access-controlled streams and assets in Livepeer Studio.
The cryptographic key holder or process that authorizes payment tickets and Ethereum transactions on behalf of a gateway node.
Lightweight component directing incoming work to the correct processing path within an orchestrator, or routing a subset of network traffic to specific orchestrators for staged rollout.
A formal commitment between a service provider and a customer defining expected performance levels, uptime guarantees, and remediation obligations.
Computational method constructing a map of an unknown environment while simultaneously tracking an agent's location within it.
A penalty mechanism that destroys a portion of an orchestrator's bonded LPT for protocol violations such as failing verification, skipping verifications, or underperformance.
The network-defined rules specifying exactly when and how LPT is slashed: failing verification checks, skipping assigned verifications, or sustained underperformance.
Self-executing program on a blockchain that automatically enforces agreement terms without intermediaries.
Preliminary test verifying that an AI pipeline or node configuration is working correctly before deploying to production or accepting live traffic.
Alpha phase of the Livepeer roadmap where the protocol was designed and incentives implemented, culminating in testnet launch.
Orchestrator deployment where a single operator runs a complete orchestrator node with all components on one machine, without pool workers.
Statically-typed, contract-oriented programming language for writing smart contracts on Ethereum and EVM-compatible chains.
Treasury-funded organizational unit with a defined scope, budget, accountability structure, and operational timeline approved via governance, used to execute specific ecosystem workstreams.
Open-source latent diffusion model for text-to-image generation, operating in a compressed latent space for efficient high-quality image synthesis.
Governance services firm serving as first GovWorks Chair, building transparent governance frameworks for Livepeer.

LPT bonded to an orchestrator through the protocol, representing a commitment that secures the network and determines the holder's proportional share of rewards, governance power, and work allocation.
An orchestrator's proportional influence in the network, determined by total bonded LPT (self-stake plus delegated stake), affecting active set rank, reward share, and governance vote weight.
A model where staking the protocol's native token is required to perform work or access network services, converting participation into token buying pressure.
A mechanism where each participant's voting power, reward allocation, or selection probability is proportional to their staked token balance rather than equal per-participant.
A governance voting system where each participant's vote weight is proportional to their bonded LPT stake.
Locking LPT tokens in a proof-of-stake protocol to participate in network security, governance, and earn inflationary rewards and fee income.
Top-level Livepeer Studio object representing a live broadcast channel, configured with a stream key, playback ID, transcoding profiles, and optional recording and multistream settings.
Secret credential used by broadcasters to authenticate and push live video to a stream's ingest endpoint; equivalent to a password for the RTMP or SRT connection.
Optimized real-time diffusion pipeline using stream batching and stochastic similarity filtering to apply generative image transformations to live video at interactive frame rates.
Performance phase upgrade introducing peer-to-peer distribution, WebRTC support, and the Orchestrator/Transcoder split.
Continuous delivery of multimedia over a network rendered in real time, as opposed to full download before playback.
Project building the video infrastructure layer for decentralized social platforms, focused on the AT Protocol ecosystem and enabling open video publishing.
Using deep neural networks to apply the visual style of one image or video to the content of another.
Video delivery with end-to-end delay under one second, typically achieved via WebRTC's UDP-based transport.
Custom open API defining how Livepeer on-chain data is indexed and queried via GraphQL, built on The Graph protocol.
The total number of LPT tokens in existence at any given time, starting from a genesis supply of 10 million and growing continuously through inflationary issuance.
Concentrated treasury spending approach targeting high-impact growth initiatives during strategic market windows.
Stability AI's latent diffusion model generating 14–25 frame video clips at 576×1024 resolution conditioned on a single input image.
Artificially generated data produced by algorithms rather than real-world events, used for training AI models when real data is scarce or sensitive.
The total potential revenue opportunity available to a product or service if it captured 100% of its target market.
The 50% participation threshold for the ratio of bonded LPT to total supply; the inflation mechanism adjusts the per-round issuance rate to push toward this target.
Liquid staking protocol for LPT allowing staking while maintaining liquidity through derivative tokens.
NVIDIA's inference SDK optimising models through quantisation, layer fusion, and kernel auto-tuning for low-latency GPU inference.
AI pipeline generating an image from a natural language text prompt using a language encoder and a diffusion model.
AI pipeline synthesising spoken audio from written text using phonetic conversion and audio synthesis models.
The mandatory waiting period of approximately 7 rounds after initiating an unbond before the freed LPT becomes withdrawable to the holder's wallet.
Reduced-size preview image representing a video frame, used for recognition, navigation, and social sharing previews.
The TicketBroker smart contract that manages Livepeer's probabilistic micropayment system, holding gateway deposits and reserves, and processing winning ticket redemptions.
Rate of successful data processing per unit time, measuring the volume of work an orchestrator can complete (segments transcoded per second, or AI requests per minute).
A smart contract mechanism that enforces a mandatory delay between when a governance proposal passes and when it can be executed on-chain.
Community orchestrator group in Western North America providing education, Start Up Grants, and pre-configured hardware for running Livepeer orchestrators.
The allocation and dispersal of LPT tokens across founders, team, investors, and the public through mechanisms including the Merkle Mine, vesting schedules, and inflationary issuance.
The economic design of a token system encompassing supply, distribution, incentives, staking, inflation, governance rights, and deflationary mechanisms.
Percentage of source segments that an orchestrator fails to transcode successfully, used as a performance and reliability metric by gateways.
Direct digital-to-digital conversion of video from one encoding format or bitrate to another for adaptive multi-rendition delivery.
A neural network architecture that uses self-attention mechanisms to process sequential data in parallel, forming the basis of most modern large language models and many vision models.
SPE seeding new contribution mechanisms, coordinating talent, and directing budgets for ecosystem workstreams.
Pool of LPT and ETH held on-chain for funding public goods and ecosystem development, governed by token holder votes via the LivepeerGovernor contract.
A governance-approved distribution of treasury funds to a specific proposal, SPE, or grant recipient.
Forum section for treasury proposals, SPE funding discussions, and resource allocation.
The on-chain process by which LPT stakeholders propose, vote on, and execute allocation of community treasury funds for ecosystem development.
Governable percentage of per-round LPT inflation diverted to the community treasury instead of being distributed directly to orchestrators and delegators (currently 10%).
Recurring community call focused on treasury discussions and SPE updates.
Beta phase of the Livepeer roadmap where LPMS supported most live streaming use cases and mainnet was deployed.
Low-latency HTTP-based streaming protocol for real-time media transport between Livepeer nodes, enabling frame-level AI processing on live streams.
System property where participants interact using cryptographic proofs rather than requiring trust in any third party.
Duration from the moment a viewer presses play to the first video frame rendered on screen; a key quality-of-experience metric for streaming performance.
Resumable file upload protocol over HTTP that allows interrupted large file uploads to resume from where they stopped rather than restarting from the beginning.
The process of initiating withdrawal of bonded LPT from an orchestrator, which triggers a 7-round waiting period (thawing period) before tokens become liquid and withdrawable.
Waiting period during which tokens are locked after initiating unbonding before becoming withdrawable (7 rounds in Livepeer).
Increasing image or video resolution using AI models that predict high-frequency detail not present in the source.
The official currency of the United States; used as the reference denomination for Livepeer gateway fees, grant amounts, treasury allocations, and market data.
A pricing configuration where work costs are denominated in US dollars, with automatic dynamic conversion to wei as the ETH/USD exchange rate fluctuates.
A US-dollar-pegged ERC-20 stablecoin issued by Tether Limited; available on some centralised exchanges as a trading pair for LPT.
A proposed mechanism that would allow LPT holders to lock tokens for an extended period in exchange for enhanced governance voting power.
Protocol-level processes that confirm orchestrators performed transcoding or AI work correctly, including Truebit-style verification and probabilistic spot-checking approaches.
A network component responsible for validating work performed by orchestrators, confirming that transcoded or AI-processed output matches expected results.
A schedule controlling when token allocations — such as founder or team grants — become available over time, often with an initial cliff period followed by pro-rata release.
A media delivery model where recorded video content is stored server-side and streamed to viewers on request at any time, in contrast to live streaming.
Audience metrics including view counts, watch time, unique viewers, and geographic distribution tracked for streams and assets.
Video on demand — system allowing users to access pre-recorded content at any time, contrasting with live streaming.
Delegators overriding their orchestrator's governance vote with their own independent stake-weighted vote on a specific proposal.
Number of rounds (1) between governance proposal creation and the start of the voting period.
Number of rounds (10) during which token holders can cast votes on a governance proposal.
The weight of a participant's vote in Livepeer on-chain governance, determined by their total bonded LPT stake at the block when the proposal was created.
Dedicated GPU memory used for storing graphics data, model weights, and intermediate tensors during AI inference.
An AI model that is already loaded into GPU memory and ready to serve inference requests immediately, without the cold-start latency of loading from storage.
Biweekly informal community call for development updates and ecosystem news.
HTTP callback mechanism where a server sends an automated POST request to a configured URL when a specified platform event occurs.
Mechanism for orchestrators to dynamically advertise their AI capabilities to gateways via HTTP webhook callbacks rather than only relying on on-chain registration.
Open-source project and W3C/IETF standard providing browsers and mobile apps with peer-to-peer real-time audio, video, and data exchange over UDP without plugins.
W3C standard format for displaying timed text (captions, subtitles, chapters, metadata) synchronized with HTML5 video playback.
The smallest denomination of Ether, where 1 ETH equals 10^18 Wei; used in on-chain price calculations and payment ticket values.
Configurable scoring parameters — including stake weight, price weight, and random selection weight — that the gateway uses to rank and select orchestrators during discovery.
IETF draft protocol enabling viewers to watch content from streaming services via WebRTC using a standardized SDP offer/answer HTTP exchange.
RFC 9725 standard protocol for WebRTC-based live video ingestion via a simple HTTP SDP offer/answer exchange, enabling browser-native broadcasting without plugins.
OpenAI's encoder-decoder transformer for speech recognition and translation, pre-trained on 680,000 hours of multilingual audio.
The configured likelihood that any given micropayment ticket is a winning ticket; a lower probability means larger face values per winning ticket.
A probabilistic payment ticket whose random outcome meets the configured win probability threshold, entitling the orchestrator to redeem it on-chain for its ETH face value.
A Livepeer node running Docker containers for AI models or transcoding processes, executing compute tasks delegated by an orchestrator.
Total amount of work assigned to an orchestrator — the aggregate of active sessions, concurrent segments, and AI inference requests being processed at a given time.
Focused execution teams organized around specific domains, translating Advisory Board recommendations into phased initiatives.
Neural network representing and predicting environment dynamics, enabling an AI agent to plan by simulating outcomes rather than acting purely from direct observation.
The annualized return on staked LPT expressed as a percentage, combining inflationary LPT rewards and any ETH fee share earned through the bonded orchestrator.
Guided learning path taking a developer from no prior knowledge of Livepeer to competent ecosystem participation through structured tutorials and exercises.
